name: Barri Booth
description_short: Barri is a financial services platform offering check cashing, money transfers, bill payment, and a prepaid Visa debit card, primarily serving Spanish-speaking communities with remittance and banking alternatives.
description_long:
Barri is an established financial services company with over 30 years of experience in money transfer and remittance services. The company operates a hybrid model combining physical store locations with a mobile app, positioning itself as an accessible financial platform for underbanked and immigrant communities, particularly those sending money to Latin America and managing finances in the United States.

Barri's core service offerings include check cashing with extended hours and competitive fees, domestic and international money transfers, bill payment services (utilities, phone, internet), mobile phone recharges across the US and Latin America, a prepaid Visa debit card for account management, and personal loans ranging from $300-$10,000 through a partnership with Oportun. Their digital presence emphasizes convenience through both in-store transactions and their mobile application available on iOS and Android platforms.

The company differentiates itself through a 4.9-star customer rating, emphasizing ease of use and reliable service delivery. Their longevity (30+ years) and specialized focus on underserved immigrant populations with Latin American connections positions them distinctly in the remittance market. The integration of multiple financial services into one app and physical location network reduces the need for customers to visit multiple providers.

Barri operates primarily as a check-cashing and money transfer service with ancillary financial products. While their loan offering expands functionality, the core business remains transaction-based rather than credit-focused. The company's strength lies in serving a specific demographic with culturally-relevant services, though their service area appears geographically limited to locations with significant Spanish-speaking populations.
address: 2601 S Kentucky Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73108

company_info.headquarters: 2601 S Kentucky Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73108

pros: Extended store hours for check cashing with competitive fees
Over 30 years of experience in money transfer and remittance services
4.9-star app rating across iOS and Android platforms
Integrated mobile app allowing money transfers, bill payment, and phone recharges from smartphone
Personal loans from $300-$10,000 available through Oportun partnership, helping build credit history
Multi-country money transfer capability to Mexico, Central America, and South America
Physical store network combined with digital options for accessibility
Prepaid Visa debit card enabling account management and bill payment consolidation
cons: Check cashing is a transaction-based service with per-use fees, not a building wealth product
Loan amounts max out at $10,000, limiting access for larger personal financing needs
Service availability appears limited to regions with significant Spanish-speaking populations and established store networks
No mention of interest rates, APRs, or detailed loan terms on public website
Company primarily serves remittance use case; limited appeal for domestic-only financial needs
best_for: Spanish-speaking immigrants sending remittances to family in Mexico, Central America, or South America
Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing check cashing, bill payment, and basic financial services
Consumers seeking small personal loans ($300-$10,000) to establish credit history
Mobile-first users wanting consolidated bill payment, recharges, and money transfer in one app
diagnosis: Barri is best for Spanish-speaking immigrants and underbanked consumers who regularly send money abroad and need convenient check cashing and bill payment services. The main caveat is that this is a transaction-based service provider, not a credit-building platform—customers should understand they're paying per-use fees rather than building equity, and loan products are limited to smaller amounts.
services: International money transfers to Latin America
Mobile phone recharges (US and Latin America)
Mobile application for iOS and Android
